openQuestions.text: Should v1.5 add optional pixel-comparison mode for PDFs, or is text-extraction-only acceptable?
Can one canonical representation cover DOCX and HTML without creating false equivalence?
Should snapshots be encrypted at rest?
Should there be a zero-JS fallback for the HTML report?
Should v2.0 support cross-format comparison (e.g., DOCX vs Markdown)?

resolvedQuestions.text: Which canonical representation and extraction pipeline best preserve semantic structure?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Markdown/mdast as canonical hub; unified.js ecosystem for multi-format AST pipeline; two-pass extraction + normalization model; format capability tiers defined.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which diff algorithm strategy best combines readable changes, structural awareness, and noise suppression?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: AST-aware structural diff (Dijkstra-based, inspired by difftastic) with word-level text fallback for leaf nodes; Myers word-diff for unsupported formats.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which automatic snapshot lifecycle is safe, atomic, recoverable?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Explicit command capture; hash-of-path identity; SHA-256 content hashing; atomic write via temp+rename; configurable retention (default: last 10 per file, 30-day TTL); explicit-pair fallback.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which local HTML report architecture is accessible, secure, self-contained?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Self-contained single .html file; CSP meta tag; dual view modes (inline + side-by-side); fidelity warning system; WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility; zero external dependencies.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which runtime, portable interface, dependency set, and standalone-skill workflow best suit v1?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Node.js/TypeScript; unified/remark/rehype ecosystem; npm package with public API; CLI for direct use; OpenCode skill wrapper for AI workflow integration.

resolvedQuestions.text: What existing libraries and products exist for document diff/compare?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: difftastic (structural diff, 25.6k stars), delta (diff renderer, 31.4k), diff-match-patch (text diff, archived), diff-so-fancy (renderer, 18.1k). No existing tool fills the standalone document diff niche.

resolvedQuestions.text: What security constraints apply to untrusted document input?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: 4-layer defense: input validation, parser hardening, output sanitization, resource limits. DOMPurify for HTML, XML bomb protection for DOCX, CSP for report output.

resolvedQuestions.text: What fixture corpus and measurable acceptance criteria should gate v1?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: ~30 fixture pairs across 6 categories; 11 measurable acceptance criteria; 10 performance benchmarks; 5 manual review cases; automated quality gate pipeline.

keyFindings.finding: Node.js/TypeScript with unified.js ecosystem is the recommended v1 runtime
Markdown/mdast is the recommended canonical intermediate format
AST-aware structural diff with word-level text fallback is the recommended diff strategy
Self-contained HTML report with CSP, fidelity warnings, and dual-view modes
Explicit-command snapshot lifecycle with atomic writes and configurable retention
4-layer defense-in-depth security model for untrusted documents
CLI Library with HTML Output (Shape C) selected over Browser SPA and Desktop App
~30 fixture pairs, 11 acceptance criteria, 10 performance benchmarks for v1 validation

ruledOutDirections.reason: Solves distributed merging, not local comparison

ruledOutDirections.approach: Pure line-based diff for structured docs
ruledOutDirections.reason: Misses structural changes, reflow false positives
Cannot support snapshot lifecycle or CLI integration
Excessive dependency weight for CLI-first use case
Violates core requirement of working without Git
